name: Vevčani Carnival (Vevčanski Karneval)
region: Southwestern North Macedonia, near Struga / Lake Ohrid
dates: January 13–14 (Julian calendar Old New Year — eve and first day of the New Year according to the old calendar)
description: One of the oldest carnivals in the world, reportedly over 1,400 years old. Held in the village of Vevčani in southwestern North Macedonia on January 13–14 (the Julian calendar New Year). The carnival is described as 'an interesting mix of paganism and modernism.' Its main characteristics are archaism, secretiveness, and improvisation. The entire village becomes a boundaryless theatre: every house and every street is a stage for disguised performers. Three traditional masks are central: the bridegroom and bride, August the Stupid, and musicians. Other masks satirise current social and political figures and phenomena — costumes are often elaborate political parodies. The role of 'August the Stupid' is especially prominent: energetic young people wear this mask and communicate through peculiar movements, gesticulations, and specific cries. Officially, women do not participate, but they do disguise — unlike men, they do not remove their masks at the end. In 1993, Vevčani and its carnival joined the World Federation of Carnival Cities, controversially enrolling under the name 'Republic of Macedonia' (later North Macedonia). Vevčani also inspired other North Macedonian towns (Strumica, Prilep) to revive their own carnival traditions. The village is noted for its red wine, which flows freely during the carnival. Participants and diaspora Vevčani residents return from around the world each January for the event. The Carnival of Vevčani has never been postponed in its recorded history.
tradition_type: Ancient Village Carnival / Julian New Year Masquerade / Political Satire Carnival
